records.ety: [F. naf, fem. nave, fr. L. nativus innate, natural, native. See Native, and cf. Naf.]

defs: Having native or unaffected simplicity; ingenuous; artless; frank; as, nave manners; a nave person; nave and unsophisticated remarks.
Having a lack of knowledge, judgment, or experience; especially, lacking sophistication in judging the motives of others; credulous; as, a naive belief in the honesty of politicians.

records.ety: [AS. nafu; akin to D. naaf, G. nabe, OHG. naba, Icel. nöf, Dan. nav, Sw. naf, Skr. nābhi nave and navel: cf. L. umbo boss of a shield. √260. Cf. Navel.]

defs: The block in the center of a wheel, from which the spokes radiate, and through which the axle passes; -- called also hub or hob.

records.ety: [F. nef, fr. L. navis ship, to which the church was often likened; akin to Gr. nay`archo`s, Skr. nāus, and perh. to AS. naca boat, G. nachen, Icel. nökkvi; cf. L. nare to swim, float. Cf. Nausea, Nautical, Naval.]

defs: The middle or body of a church, extending from the transepts to the principal entrances, or, if there are no transepts, from the choir to the principal entrance, but not including the aisles.
